answer:
The last time I heard about something unfair was when my friend got passed over for a promotion at work, even though he had way more experience than the guy who got it. I guess the other guy was buddies with the manager, and that's why he was chosen instead. It really bugged me to hear about that situation. 

On one hand, it taught me that life isn't always fair and you can't control other people's poor decisions. But it also made me think about times when I have the chance to make sure things are fair, like when I'm in charge of a team or project. I realized I need to always consider people's skills and character when making decisions, not just personal relationships.

If I had the power, I would have approached the manager to vouch for my friend's talents and work ethic. I could have explained all the ways he was the most qualified for the promotion over the other guy. And I would have asked the manager to really reflect on his choice and do what was right for the company, not just his friend.

I think speaking up when you see unfairness is important, even if you can't force the outcome. At least I could have told my friend I did everything I could to defend him. Going forward, I want to be more aware of biased decisions at work and in life. If I'm ever in a position to prevent unfairness, I hope I'll have the courage to do so. This situation was disappointing but also a good lesson in advocating for what's right.
title: Describe the last time you heard about something that you thought was unfair. What did you learn from this situation? If you had the power to change it, what would you have done differently?
extData:
{"answerTemplate":"本题模板:

开场陈述:
开始时描述你最近听说的一个你认为不公平的情况: "Recently, I encountered a situation that struck me as particularly unfair, which involved [briefly describe the unfair situation, e.g., a workplace incident, a policy decision, a social issue]."

不公平情况的细节:
描述这个不公平情况的具体内容: "The issue at hand was [explain the situation in more detail, focusing on why it seemed unjust, like unequal treatment, abuse of power, disregard for rights]."
强调情况的影响: "This incident was troubling because it affected [mention who was impacted and how, such as employees, certain community members, a vulnerable group]."
从这个情况中学到的东西:
讨论你从这个情况中学到的东西: "From this situation, I learned that [discuss the insights or lessons gained, such as the importance of speaking up, understanding systemic issues, recognizing one's own biases]."
描述这个经历的启示: "It highlighted [mention any broader societal or ethical issues it brought to light, like the need for more inclusive policies, the value of empathy and understanding]."
如何改变这个情况:
如果你有能力改变它,你会做什么: "Given the power to change this situation, I would have [explain what actions you would take, such as implementing fairer policies, raising awareness, advocating for those affected]."
描述你的改变可能带来的影响: "By making these changes, the outcome could have been [discuss the potential positive impact of your actions, like a more equitable environment, better morale, justice served]."
结论:
总结你对这个不公平情况的看法和学习: "In conclusion, while the situation I witnessed was unjust, it served as an important lesson in [summarize your main takeaway, like the importance of fairness, the power of collective action]."
结束语: "Understanding and addressing such injustices is crucial for creating a more equitable and compassionate society.""}
